title: The Tidal Library

features.name: chart-shelf
features.desc-on-touch: laminated navigation charts rest here, their pencilled routes still visible: courses sailed, tides recorded, reefs marked where they killed ships. the archive has filed them as testimony.

features.name: lighthouse-signal
features.desc-on-touch: a faint glow marks the keeper's tower connection. signals travel along this reef: light becomes data, data becomes story, story becomes why ships remember this place.

features.name: tidal-marking-stone
features.desc-on-touch: carved with tide heights at different moons, different years. navigation is testimony: knowing the water's mood is knowing the place's memory.

features.name: sailor-testimony-pool
features.desc-on-touch: a shallow pool where charts were left to dry, and rain has washed them with other people's notes. safe passage, danger here, saw something alive that the charts don't name.

features.name: reef-reading-station
features.desc-on-touch: where the tidal library ends and the actual reef begins. the archive reads the rock the same way it reads the charts: as a text written by time and difficulty.

inscription: the charts learned to speak the language of the rocks, and the rocks learned to speak back through the sailors who brought the charts

file.name: Coastal_Navigation_Charts_Annotated_Tidal_Testimony_1950s_Present.txt
file.note: official charts, margin-notes in multiple hands, tide-table extracts, a log entry about a ship that used this knowledge to survive, and another about one that didn't

exits.label: back along the lighthouse-edge to the tower's keep
further along the reef, where charts become less useful and the reef teaches its own language
downward, toward the deep reef where no chart reaches
exits.to: NEW: the unmapped reef where testimony ends and the stone begins

exits.label: back toward shore, where the keeper's light reaches furthest
